hyperedges.label: TV Tizen Sync Flow (features/ui build to tizen-index.html to tizen/ output)
apps/web Build Dependency Chain (domain to ui/features to web)
Docker Compose Deployment Stack (api + web services)

nodes.label: Vitest Workspace + MockAgent Test Harness
nodes.rationale: Establishes vitest infra and undici MockAgent for Jellyfin/Seerr as the reusable pattern for all subsequent slices' integration tests, starting with crypto round-trip unit tests as the first passing suite.

nodes.label: Lolarr Hybrid-BFF Architecture
nodes.rationale: Metadata/Auth/Seerr traffic goes through apps/api (Fastify); images and video streams go client-direct to Jellyfin later, so the BFF never becomes a media-proxy bottleneck.

nodes.label: Demo-Mode Removal
nodes.rationale: Demo data/fallbacks (demoUser, demoRows, findDemoItem, searchDemoItems) are removed entirely; API must fail fast at startup without required config, so there is exactly one code path and no risk of silently serving fake data in production.

nodes.label: Silent Quick Connect (Seerr session renewal)
nodes.rationale: Whenever the Seerr session is missing/expired (401) and a valid Jellyfin token exists, the BFF re-authenticates transparently via Seerr's QuickConnect endpoints and the user's stored Jellyfin token — avoiding admin-key impersonation and giving a single Seerr auth path for both QC-login and cookie renewal.

nodes.label: 401-Kaskade (session invalidation cascade)
nodes.rationale: If the Jellyfin token itself is invalid (e.g. admin revoke), the BFF ends the Lolarr session and forces the client back to login; a Seerr 401 instead triggers Silent-QC renewal first, only cascading to logout if that also fails.

nodes.label: SEERR_API_KEY scoping (admin key vs per-user session)
nodes.rationale: SEERR_API_KEY is used only for cacheable, user-independent reads (discover/search/media-details); writes (create/delete requests) always go through the per-user Seerr session, preventing the admin key from being used to impersonate users.
nodes.norm_label: seerr_api_key scoping (admin key vs per-user session)

nodes.label: MediaItem.jellyfin sub-object (Approach A)
nodes.rationale: Chosen approach: one MediaItem type with an optional jellyfin sub-object rather than a discriminated union or type split, keeping Discover (TMDB) and Library (Jellyfin) items unified under one schema.

nodes.label: Home Degradation Strategy (Promise.allSettled row-wise)
nodes.rationale: Partial Jellyfin failures drop only the affected row with a warn-log; total Jellyfin failure yields Discover-only home; Seerr failure yields Jellyfin-only home; 502 jellyfin_unreachable is returned only when zero rows could be produced and at least one source failed.

nodes.label: Discover 5-minute In-Memory Cache
nodes.rationale: Seerr discover results are cached in-memory for 5 minutes, user-independent, so a warm cache masks brief Seerr outages (data younger than 5 minutes is treated as fresh).

nodes.label: DirectPlay -> Transcode Retry Rule
nodes.rationale: If the player fires an error event while a direct-play source is loading/playing, exactly one retry occurs with enableDirectPlay:false; a second failure (or any hls error) goes straight to an error state — bounded retries avoid infinite loops while covering the common DirectPlay-incompatibility case (Moonfin pattern).

nodes.label: ProgressReporter (10s interval + event-driven reporting)
nodes.rationale: Reports progress every 10s plus immediately on pause/resume/seek; uses navigator.sendBeacon on pagehide as an unload-safety net since fetch may be cancelled on tab close.

nodes.label: "Toast exactly once" Dedup Strategy
nodes.rationale: Server persists/dedupes notifications with a stable id (unique on user+tmdb+media_type+kind); client seeds a Set of already-seen ids on first snapshot without toasting (backlog becomes badge-only), then toasts each new id exactly once, keeping read-state server-side/cross-device while toast display stays per-device.

nodes.label: Poll (~45s) instead of SSE/WebSocket Realtime Push
nodes.rationale: Media-available events are minute-scale, so SSE/WebSocket complexity (especially on the Tizen webview) isn't justified for V1; a lightweight dedicated poll suffices and is upgradeable later.
nodes.norm_label: poll (~45s) instead of sse/websocket realtime push

nodes.label: Username-based User Matching (no email fallback)
nodes.rationale: requestedBy_username is matched case-insensitively against users.name because Seerr requires Jellyfin auth (guaranteed by the Silent-QC flow); there is no email field in the users table, so renamed Seerr display names are a documented risk accepted for V1.

nodes.label: Seerr as Source of Truth for Requests
nodes.rationale: The local SQLite requests table and database.createRequest/listRequests are removed; all request data is fetched live from Seerr through the per-user session, avoiding drift between two sources of request state.
nodes.norm_label: seerr as source of truth for requests

nodes.label: Seerr Request/Media Status Mapping Table
nodes.rationale: Combines Seerr's request.status (pending/approved/declined/failed) with media.status (available/processing/partial) into a single domain RequestStatus enum so the UI shows one coherent status per request rather than two overlapping upstream fields.

nodes.label: PlayerPlatform Injection Seam
nodes.rationale: Replaces the hardcoded WebPlayer + threaded videoRef with an injected PlayerPlatform object (createPlayer/buildDeviceProfile/supportsVolume/registerMediaKeys), mirroring the existing TvAction/TvTextInput adapter-injection pattern, so the full playback-session orchestration from Slice 3 is reused across web and Tizen.

nodes.label: DTS/DCA Always-Transcode Rule
nodes.rationale: DTS/DCA audio is excluded from every Tizen device profile year because it is unsupported on every Tizen firmware generation, forcing a transcode rather than a failed DirectPlay attempt.

nodes.label: AVPlay 500ms Polling for timeupdate
nodes.rationale: AVPlay's oncurrentplaytime callback is unreliable (per Moonfin's verified source), so timeupdate is instead emitted via a 500ms setInterval polling getState()+getCurrentTime().

hyperedges.label: TV Tizen Sync Flow (features/ui build to tizen-index.html to tizen/ output)
apps/web Build Dependency Chain (domain to ui/features to web)
Docker Compose Deployment Stack (api + web services)
